 {"id":"bd-3hb3","title":"E2E: install/update/rollback scripts with detailed logging","description":"## Goal
Add comprehensive E2E scripts for install.sh + update + rollback with verbose logging.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Containerized install tests (clean system, verify success).
- Tampered binary/invalid checksum failure path.
- Update + rollback flows with version verification.
- JSONL logs + checksums + timing metrics captured as artifacts.
- Cross-platform matrix where possible (linux/mac/windows runners).

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL schema fields: event, timestamp, phase, command, exit_code, duration_ms, checksums, artifacts[].
- Artifact bundle: logs + checksums + resolved version info.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] E2E install scripts pass across supported platforms/containers.
- [ ] Fail paths are validated and logged clearly.
- [ ] Artifacts include logs + checksums + versions.
- [ ] JSONL schema validation passes.

## Test Plan
- E2E: docker-based runs for install/update/rollback.
- Logging: JSONL schema validation + artifact manifest validation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:23.212974556Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:21.449151353Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hb3","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:21.449114695Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3hb3","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:23.220380171Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3hb3","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:30.967761891Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3hb3","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:23.228792047Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3hb3","depends_on_id":"process_triage-ica.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:23.234889641Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3jpe","title":"Add differential and goal-oriented options to agent plan","description":"## Task
Add differential and goal-oriented option parsing to `pt agent plan` for API surface harmonization.

**SCOPE LIMITATION**: This bead implements CLI flag parsing only. Full feature implementation is deferred to:
- Differential mode: process_triage-9k8.2 (EPIC: Phase 11 - Differential Analysis)
- Goal-oriented: process_triage-95m.* (EPIC: Phase 13 - Goal-Oriented Optimization)
- Predictions: process_triage-yxt.* (EPIC: Phase 12 - Trajectory Prediction)

## Current Implementation
```
pt agent plan [OPTIONS]
  --session <id>           Resume existing session
  --max-candidates <N>     Maximum candidates
  --min-posterior <f64>    Minimum posterior (alias: --threshold)
  --only <filter>          Filter by recommendation
  --deep                   Force deep scan
  --min-age <seconds>      Minimum process age
```

## New Options (Stub Implementation for v1)

### Differential Mode Flags
```
--since <session-id>       Compare against prior session
--since-time <ts|dur>      Compare against time (e.g., "2h", "2026-01-15T12:00:00Z")
```

### Goal-Oriented Flags
```
--goal "<target>"          Resource recovery goal (format: "free 4GB RAM")
```

### Predictive Mode Flags
```
--include-predictions      Add trajectory analysis to output
```

## Implementation Strategy

### Phase 1 (This Bead): Parse flags, return not-implemented warning
```rust
// Add to AgentPlanArgs
#[arg(long, help = "Compare against prior session (coming in v1.2)")]
since: Option<String>,

#[arg(long, help = "Compare against time (coming in v1.2)")]
since_time: Option<String>,

#[arg(long, help = "Resource recovery goal (coming in v1.2)")]
goal: Option<String>,

#[arg(long, help = "Add trajectory analysis (coming in v1.2)")]
include_predictions: bool,
```

### Behavior for v1 (Stub)
When any of these flags are used, the plan is generated normally BUT includes a warning:

```json
{
  "session_id": "pt-20260122-...",
  "candidates": [...],
  "stub_flags": {
    "warning": "feature_not_implemented",
    "message": "Some flags are parsed but not yet functional",
    "flags_used": ["--since"],
    "flags_ignored": ["--since"],
    "workaround": "Use pt agent diff for manual comparison",
    "planned_release": "v1.2"
  }
}
```

**Key behaviors:**
- Exit code: 0 (success with warning, not error)
- Plan is still generated (flags are ignored, not blocking)
- Warning appears in both JSON and summary formats
- Agents can discover the API surface via --help
- Scripts using these flags degrade gracefully

### Phase 2 (Future Beads): Full implementation
- Differential: Depends on session comparison infrastructure
- Goal-oriented: Depends on optimization engine
- Predictions: Depends on trajectory modeling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--since`, `--since-time`, `--goal`, `--include-predictions` flags parse correctly
- [ ] Using these flags returns normal plan WITH warning, not error
- [ ] Help text documents flags with "(coming in v1.2)" notes
- [ ] Exit code 0 even when using stub flags
- [ ] Plan output unchanged when flags not used
- [ ] Warning in both JSON and summary formats
- [ ] `stub_flags` section clearly lists which flags were used and ignored

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_plan_parses_since_flag() {
    // --since value is captured
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_parses_goal_flag() {
    // --goal "free 4GB RAM" is captured
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_stub_flags_produces_warning() {
    // Using --since results in stub_flags warning in output
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_stub_flags_still_generates_plan() {
    // Plan candidates still present when stub flags used
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_no_warning_without_stub_flags() {
    // Normal plan without stub flags has no stub_flags section
}
```

### E2E Test Script (test/e2e_plan_future_flags.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test for plan command future flags (stub implementation)

set -euo pipefail
LOG="test/logs/e2e_plan_future_flags_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== E2E Test: plan future flags (stubs) ==="

# Test --since flag parsing
log "Testing --since flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --since "fake-session" --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

log "Exit code: $EXIT_CODE"

# Verify exit code is 0 (success with warning)
if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ Exit code 0 (success with warning)"
else
    log "⚠ Exit code $EXIT_CODE (may be acceptable if error is clear)"
fi

# Verify warning is present
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags.warning == \"feature_not_implemented\"" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --since returns not-implemented warning"
    USED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".stub_flags.flags_used | join(\", \")")
    log "  Flags used: $USED"
elif echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".warning" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Warning present (different structure)"
else
    log "✗ --since should return warning"
    echo "$OUTPUT" | head -20 >> "$LOG"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify plan is still generated
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".session_id or .candidates" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Plan still generated despite stub flag"
else
    log "⚠ Plan might not be generated (check output)"
fi

# Test --goal flag parsing  
log "Testing --goal flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --goal "free 4GB RAM" --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ --goal accepted (exit 0)"
else
    log "⚠ --goal exit code $EXIT_CODE"
fi

if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags or .warning" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --goal returns warning"
else
    log "✗ --goal should return warning"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --include-predictions flag
log "Testing --include-predictions flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --include-predictions --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ --include-predictions accepted (exit 0)"
else
    log "⚠ --include-predictions exit code $EXIT_CODE"
fi

if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags or .warning" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --include-predictions returns warning"
else
    log "✗ --include-predictions should return warning"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --since-time flag
log "Testing --since-time flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --since-time "2h" --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ --since-time accepted (exit 0)"
else
    log "⚠ --since-time exit code $EXIT_CODE"
fi

# Test normal plan has no warning
log "Testing normal plan (no stub flags)..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "⚠ Normal plan has stub_flags (should not)"
else
    log "✓ Normal plan has no stub_flags section"
fi

# Test help mentions stub flags
log "Testing help output..."
HELP=$(pt-core agent plan --help 2>&1)
if echo "$HELP" | grep -q "since"; then
    log "✓ Help mentions --since"
else
    log "✗ Help should mention --since"
    exit 1
fi

if echo "$HELP" | grep -qi "coming\|v1.2\|future\|planned"; then
    log "✓ Help indicates feature is upcoming"
else
    log "⚠ Help might not indicate feature is upcoming"
fi

log "=== E2E Test PASSED ==="
```","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltBeaver","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:38:26.764582930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:15:28.652172776Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3jpe","depends_on_id":"bd-v5ba","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:54Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-3jpe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented stub flags for --since, --since-time, --goal, --include-predictions. Plan generates normally with stub_flags warning section. Updated docs.","created_at":"2026-01-23T02:42:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3k9b","title":"E2E: bundle encryption + verification flows","description":"## Goal
Validate bundle encryption/decryption and checksum verification end-to-end with detailed logging and failure-path coverage.

## Scope
- `pt bundle create --encrypt` with passphrase arg and env var.
- `pt bundle inspect` + `extract` with verification on/off.
- Failure modes: wrong passphrase, corrupted bundle, missing manifest.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: encrypted bundle, inspect output, extract output, manifest, stderr snapshots.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Encrypted bundles decrypt with correct passphrase only.
- [ ] Verification detects corruption.
- [ ] Failure paths produce clear errors + correct exit codes.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted per case.

## Test Plan
- E2E script generates bundle from fixture session and exercises encrypt/decrypt/verify.

-{"id":"bd-3kk2","title":"No-mock inference + ledger regression suite","description":"## Goal
Build a no-mock, deterministic regression suite for inference/posterior math and the evidence ledger.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Capture **realistic evidence fixtures** from live runs (CPU/IO/TTY/orphan/parent/cgroup/net), store as versioned test data.
- End-to-end inference pipeline on fixtures (collection features -> posterior -> confidence -> ledger), **no mocks**.
- Golden outputs for:
  - posterior class scores + confidence labels
  - top evidence factors with contributions and ordering
  - any derived summaries used by agent/TUI outputs
- Property tests:
  - posterior normalization (sum≈1)
  - monotonicity for key signals (e.g., longer idle increases P(abandoned))
  - sensitivity to missing evidence (graceful degradation)
- Numeric stability regression tests (NaN/Inf handling, underflow protection).

## No-mock Policy (Commentary)
- Use recorded fixtures and **actual inference code paths**; no trait mocks or fake implementations.
- Deterministic seeds and fixed fixtures for repeatability.
- Golden output updates require explicit approval (guardrail against drifting expectations).

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs per test case: fixture_id, posterior summary, top factors, confidence labels, runtime.
- Log schema fields: event, timestamp, phase, case_id, command, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Redact/normalize any sensitive command strings in logs.
- Store artifacts under `test/artifacts/inference/<case>/` for CI upload.

## E2E Coverage
- Add `test/inference_e2e.bats` that runs `pt agent plan` on a fixture session and validates output schema + ledger summary.
- E2E uses runner harness (process_triage-aii.7.7) and emits JSONL logs using the shared schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Inference on fixtures produces stable outputs (golden tests).
- [ ] Posterior outputs normalized and within defined tolerance.
- [ ] Evidence ledger ordering and contributions validated.
- [ ] Failure modes (NaN/Inf/empty evidence) covered deterministically.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted per test case with stable schema.
- [ ] E2E fixture plan run validates output schema + ledger summary.

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden inference cases + property checks.
- Integration: pipeline from fixture -> features -> posterior -> ledger.
- E2E: fixture plan run with JSONL logging.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:11.073333494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:29:35.298749454Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:27.429597853Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:11.086052876Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:11.093432894Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:21.790324959Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3kk2","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:45:24.660814698Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3p7w","title":"E2E coverage remaining surfaces (shadow/diff/telemetry/config/bundle-enc)","description":"## Goal
Fill remaining end-to-end integration coverage gaps with rich JSONL logging + artifacts.

## Scope (Child Tasks)
- Shadow mode lifecycle (start/run/stop/export/report).
- Session diff/compare workflows (pt diff + agent diff).
- Telemetry maintenance (status/prune/retention).
- Config preset workflows (list/show/diff/export/validate).
- Bundle encryption/verification flows (passphrase env + CLI).
- Output formatting modes (fields/compact/TOON/truncation).
- Capabilities manifest + cache flows (agent capabilities).
- Supervision gating + protected patterns (plan/action guardrails).
- Policy guardrails (rate limits, blast radius, max kills).
- Degraded environment + permissions (missing deps, permission errors, unsupported features).

## Logging & Artifacts
- All E2E scripts emit JSONL logs with shared schema + artifact manifests.
- Failure-mode coverage required (invalid inputs, missing files, wrong passphrase, malformed policy).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each listed surface has deterministic E2E coverage in CI.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate for every script.
- [ ] Negative-paths are explicitly validated for each surface.

 {"id":"bd-3t22","title":"E2E: capabilities manifest + cache","description":"## Goal
Validate agent capabilities manifest generation + cache behavior end-to-end.

## Scope
- `pt agent capabilities --format json` schema validation.
- Cache hit/miss behavior (first run vs second run) + TTL/refresh behavior.
- Negative paths: unreadable cache dir, invalid cache contents, missing manifest.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing errors are clear and actionable.
- Consume fixtures produced by bd-1591 + bd-z3ma.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, cache_hit, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: raw outputs, schema validation reports, stderr snapshots, manifest.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Capabilities output is schema-valid and deterministic.
- [ ] Cache behavior validated across runs (hit/miss/refresh).
- [ ] Error paths produce clear messages + correct exit codes.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate.

## Test Plan
- E2E script via shared harness with fixtures.

@@ -352,7 +352,7 @@
 {"id":"process_triage-iaco","title":"Implement pt agent export-priors command","description":"**Purpose**: Implement the `pt agent export-priors` command for transferring learned priors between machines.

**Plan Document Reference**: Section 3.5.1 (8. Learning)

**CLI Surface**:
```
pt agent export-priors --out priors.json [OPTIONS]
```

**Required Options**:
- `--out <path>` - Output file path
- `--host-profile <name>` - Tag priors with machine characteristics for smart matching

**Exported Content**:
- Beta/Gamma/Dirichlet hyperparameters learned from this machine
- Hazard rate priors by regime
- Category-specific priors (test runners, dev servers, agents, etc.)
- Calibration metadata (shadow mode observations, false-kill rates)
- Host profile characteristics (CPU count, memory, typical workloads)

**Use Cases**:
- Bootstrap new machines from experienced ones
- Fleet-wide shared priors with versioning
- Transfer learning across similar host types
- Backup/restore of learned state

**Integration Points**:
- Works with `pt agent import-priors` for importing
- Used by fleet mode for learning transfer (Phase 14)
- Supports per-machine baseline system (Section 4.45)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the described scope with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).
- [ ] Includes unit/integration/E2E coverage for the highest-risk paths.

 {"id":"process_triage-iau","title":"EPIC: Phase 2 - Math Utilities Foundation","description":"## Overview
This epic covers the implementation of all mathematical primitives needed by the Bayesian inference engine. These are the building blocks that make the 'alien artifact' inference possible.

## Background & Context
The inference engine (Section 4) uses closed-form Bayesian computation throughout:
- Beta-Binomial for CPU occupancy
- Beta-Bernoulli for binary features (orphan, TTY, net)
- Dirichlet-Multinomial for categorical features
- Gamma for hazard rates and runtime
- Bayes factors for model comparison

All math must be numerically stable, auditable, and efficient.

## Why This Matters
- **Correctness**: Numerical instability leads to wrong classifications
- **Auditability**: Galaxy-brain mode requires exact math display
- **Performance**: Inference runs on every process; math must be fast
- **Testability**: Math functions are deterministic and unit-testable

## Scope
1. Beta distribution utilities (PDF, CDF, inverse CDF)
2. Beta-Binomial posterior predictive
3. Beta-Bernoulli posterior predictive
4. Dirichlet-Multinomial posterior predictive
5. Gamma PDF and posterior updates
6. Log-sum-exp and other stability primitives
7. Bayes factor computation
8. Log-odds and posterior computation

## Success Criteria
- All functions pass unit tests with known values
- Numerical stability verified for extreme inputs
- Performance benchmarks meet requirements (<1ms per process for core primitives)
- Functions match documented equations exactly

## Technical Constraints
- Pure Rust implementation for core primitives
- Use log-domain everywhere to prevent underflow
- Consider SIMD for batched operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All child math beads are implemented with documented APIs and unit tests.
- [ ] Property-based tests cover invariants (normalization, monotonicity, inversion where applicable).
- [ ] Numerical stability primitives are reused throughout (no ad-hoc reimplementations in inference code).

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden-value tests for each distribution and update.
- Property: proptest/quickcheck for invariants and randomized cross-checks.
- Perf: micro-benchmarks for hot-path math routines.

Phase 2 Math Foundation complete.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-iau","depends_on_id":"process_triage-2l3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"process_triage-iau","depends_on_id":"process_triage-h89","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-ic9d","title":"Implement confidence visualization","description":"## Overview
Implement **confidence/uncertainty visualization** for decisions in the premium TUI (Plan §7.2–§7.3).

This is not a cosmetic feature: it is how the system communicates uncertainty and earns trust.

## What We Need to Visualize
### 1) Posterior uncertainty
- Show key posterior probabilities (e.g., `P(abandoned)`, `P(useful_bad)`), not a single opaque “score”.
- When applicable, show credible intervals / uncertainty summaries (e.g., from Beta/Gamma posteriors and shadow-mode calibration bounds).

Example (textual):
```
P(abandoned)=0.92  (credible interval shown when available)
Expected loss: keep=28.2, pause=15.1, kill=6.4
```

### 2) Decision robustness
- Indicate whether the recommendation is sensitive to small evidence changes (“knife-edge” decisions).
- Surface robustness gate status:
  - PPC/drift warnings
  - DRO / robust Bayes tightening
  - e-process / FDR budget constraints

### 3) Calibration status
- If shadow-mode calibration exists for this host/profile, show whether the model is currently in a calibrated regime.
- If outside calibrated regime, show a “caution” indicator and encourage REVIEW.

### 4) Evidence contribution visualization
Use the evidence ledger outputs (Bayes factors/log contributions) to visualize *why*, e.g.:
- top 3 evidence contributors
- “residual/other” bucket

## Visual Language (TUI)
- Badges: SAFE / CAUTION / DANGER
- Confidence: HIGH / MED / LOW (derived from posterior concentration + calibration state + robustness gates)
- Explicitly separate:
  - probability (belief)
  - expected loss (decision)
  - gates (permission)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users can quickly distinguish high-confidence vs low-confidence recommendations.
- [ ] Visualization reflects posterior + expected loss + gates (not ad-hoc thresholds).
- [ ] Redaction-safe rendering (no leaking sensitive raw strings).

## Test Plan
- Unit: mapping from numeric summaries → discrete badge states.
- Integration: fixture sessions produce stable visual outputs.

-{"id":"process_triage-ica","title":"EPIC: Release, Packaging, and Documentation","description":"## Overview
Release, packaging, and documentation work needed to ship the “alien artifact” system as a trustworthy product.

This is **not** one of the numbered implementation phases in the plan; it is a cross-cutting deliverables epic aligned with Plan §12 (Deliverables).

## Plan References
- Plan §3.0 (execution/packaging architecture)
- Plan §12 (Deliverables)

## Scope
1. User documentation (README, tutorials, manpages)
2. Developer documentation (architecture, contributing, design rationale)
3. Agent/fleet documentation (schema reference, error handling, exit codes)
4. Packaging and distribution (pt wrapper + pt-core artifacts)
5. Release automation, checksums, and provenance

## Quality Bar
- “Install and run” should be a one-command, low-friction experience.
- Artifacts should be verifiable (checksums; optional signatures).
- Docs should make the system auditable: evidence → posterior → expected loss → action → outcome.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Documentation covers all user-visible behaviors and safety constraints.
- [ ] Packaging produces repeatable artifacts for supported platforms.
- [ ] Release pipeline enforces version consistency and publishes checksums.

 {"id":"process_triage-ica.1","title":"EPIC: Advanced Installation, Distribution, and Auto-Update","description":"## Overview
Enhance installation, distribution, and auto-update mechanisms with modern best practices for cross-platform CLI tools.

## Background
The current installation infrastructure (process_triage-n0r, process_triage-097, process_triage-aip) covers basic functionality. This epic adds advanced features based on 2025/2026 research into CLI tool distribution best practices.

## Research Findings (January 2026)

### Cross-Platform Distribution with cross-rs
- Docker/Podman containers for zero-setup cross-compilation
- Supported targets: linux-musl (portable), windows-gnu, macOS (requires native)
- GitHub Actions integration via \`actions-rust-cross\`
- Recommendation: Use cross for Linux builds, native for macOS/Windows

### Self-Update Best Practices (go-selfupdate patterns)
1. **Semantic Versioning**: Git tags as version source (v1.2.3 or 1.2.3)
2. **Checksum Validation**: SHA256 with \`.sha256\` suffix files
3. **Signature Verification**: ECDSA signatures with \`.sig\` suffix
4. **Rollback Support**: Backup binary before update, restore on failure
5. **Goreleaser Integration**: Single \`checksums.txt\` for all assets
6. **Archive Formats**: Support zip, tar.gz, tar.xz

### Package Manager Integration
1. **Homebrew**: tap repository with formula/cask
2. **Scoop**: bucket repository with manifest
3. **cargo-binstall**: Binary installation from crates.io metadata
4. **apt/deb**: Debian repository with signed packages
5. **AUR**: Arch User Repository package

### Idempotent Installation Patterns
- Check existing version before download
- Atomic file replacement (write temp, rename)
- Post-install verification (run --version)
- Cleanup on failure (no partial state)
- XDG compliance for all paths

## Scope

### 1. Enhanced Cross-Compilation Pipeline
- Linux: x86_64-unknown-linux-musl + aarch64-unknown-linux-musl
- macOS: x86_64-apple-darwin + aarch64-apple-darwin (Universal Binary)
- Windows: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc + aarch64-pc-windows-msvc
- GitHub Actions matrix with cross-rs for Linux
- Native builds for macOS/Windows in CI

### 2. Cryptographic Verification
- ECDSA signatures for all release binaries
- \`.sig\` files alongside binaries
- Verification in installer: \`--verify\` flag (default on)
- Key distribution via keybase/well-known URI
- Checksum file with SHA256 hashes (\`checksums.txt\`)

### 3. Rollback-Safe Self-Update
- Backup current binary to \`~/.cache/process_triage/rollback/\`
- Atomic update via temp file + rename
- Version verification after update
- Automatic rollback on verification failure
- \`pt update --rollback\` to restore previous version

### 4. Package Manager Manifests
- homebrew-tap repository with formula
- scoop-bucket repository with manifest
- cargo-binstall metadata in Cargo.toml
- AUR PKGBUILD (community maintained)
- Generated automatically from release workflow

### 5. Dependency Auto-Installation
- Detect missing required tools (gum, jq)
- Prompt for auto-install with package manager
- Support: apt, brew, dnf, pacman, scoop
- Skip if already installed (idempotent)
- Optional \`--no-deps\` to skip dependency check

### 6. Agent Auto-Configuration
- Detect installed coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Copilot)
- Offer to add pt to agent tool paths
- Create agent-specific configuration snippets
- \`pt install --configure-agents\` flag

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/src/install/update_test.rs\`
- **Coverage target**: 95% for security-critical paths
- Test cases:
  - ECDSA signature verification (valid/invalid/missing)
  - SHA256 checksum validation
  - Atomic file replacement (crash recovery)
  - Version comparison (semver parsing)
  - Rollback restoration
  - Package manager detection

### Integration Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/tests/install_integration.rs\`
- Test cases:
  - Download from mock GitHub releases API
  - Signature verification with test keys
  - Rollback mechanism (corrupt binary scenario)
  - Dependency detection for each package manager

### E2E Tests
- **File**: \`test/install_e2e.bats\`
- Test scenarios:
  - \`install.sh\` succeeds on clean system (Docker container)
  - \`install.sh --verify\` fails on tampered binary
  - \`pt update\` downloads and installs new version
  - \`pt update --rollback\` restores previous version
  - Idempotent: running install twice produces same state
- **Artifact logging**: Full install log with timing and checksums

### Cross-Platform Tests
- **File**: \`.github/workflows/install-test.yml\`
- Matrix: ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest
- Container tests: debian, alpine, fedora, arch
- ARM64: ubuntu-arm64-latest, macos-arm64

### Security Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/tests/install_security.rs\`
- Test cases:
  - Reject binary with wrong signature
  - Reject binary with wrong checksum
  - Reject binary from untrusted source
  - Verify key pinning works
  - Time-of-check/time-of-use resistance

---

## Logging Specifications

### Structured Log Events
| Event | Level | Fields | Purpose |
|-------|-------|--------|---------|
| \`install.start\` | INFO | version, target, source_url | Install begins |
| \`install.download\` | INFO | url, size_bytes, duration_ms | Download complete |
| \`install.verify_checksum\` | INFO | expected, actual, match | Checksum check |
| \`install.verify_signature\` | INFO | key_id, valid | Signature check |
| \`install.backup\` | INFO | path, backup_path | Backup created |
| \`install.replace\` | INFO | old_version, new_version | Binary replaced |
| \`install.rollback\` | WARN | reason, restored_version | Rollback triggered |
| \`install.complete\` | INFO | version, duration_ms | Install finished |
| \`install.error\` | ERROR | stage, error_message, recoverable | Failure |

### Audit Trail
- All install/update operations logged to \`~/.config/process_triage/install.log\`
- Include: timestamp, version, outcome, verification status
- Retained for 90 days minimum

---

## Error Handling

### Failure Modes
| Scenario | Detection | Recovery | User Message |
|----------|-----------|----------|--------------|
| Network failure | HTTP error/timeout | Retry 3x, then fail | "Download failed. Check network and retry." |
| Checksum mismatch | SHA256 comparison | Abort install | "Checksum verification failed! Binary may be corrupted." |
| Signature invalid | ECDSA verify fails | Abort install | "Signature verification failed! Binary not from trusted source." |
| Disk full | Write fails ENOSPC | Cleanup and abort | "Not enough disk space. Need Xmb free." |
| Permission denied | Write fails EACCES | Suggest sudo | "Cannot write to PATH. Try: sudo pt update" |
| Binary corrupt | Version check fails | Rollback | "Update failed verification. Rolling back to previous version." |

### Rollback Procedure
1. Detect failure (binary won't run, wrong version, etc.)
2. Log failure reason with full context
3. Copy backup binary back to install location
4. Verify restored version runs correctly
5. Report rollback to user with failure reason

---

## Performance Targets
- Download: Limited by network (show progress bar)
- Verification: < 500ms for checksum + signature
- Atomic replace: < 100ms
- Rollback: < 200ms
- Total install time: < 30s on reasonable network

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Musl binaries for portable Linux
- [ ] Universal binary for macOS
- [ ] ECDSA signatures verified by default
- [ ] Rollback mechanism works
- [ ] Homebrew tap published and tested
- [ ] Scoop bucket published and tested
- [ ] cargo-binstall metadata added
- [ ] Dependencies auto-installed with consent
- [ ] Agent configuration offered during install
- [ ] Unit tests pass with 95%+ coverage on security paths
- [ ] E2E tests pass on all platforms in CI
- [ ] Logging meets specification above

## Dependencies
- Parent: process_triage-ica (Release, Packaging, and Documentation)
- Depends on: GitHub Actions CI/CD (process_triage-68c)
- Depends on: Testing infrastructure (process_triage-aii)
- Creates: Cross-platform test workflow (process_triage-ica.1.t1)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:16.842746438Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:04:56.199031099Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"process_triage-ica.1.1","title":"Implement ECDSA signature verification for releases","description":"## Overview
Add cryptographic signature verification to ensure binary integrity beyond checksums.

## Background
SHA256 checksums verify integrity but not authenticity. ECDSA signatures prove the binary came from a trusted source.

## Research Context (go-selfupdate patterns)
- ECDSA signatures stored as \`.sig\` files
- Public key distributed via:
  - GitHub releases description
  - .well-known/pt-signing-key
  - Keybase identity
- Verification happens before installation

## Scope

### 1. Key Generation and Management
- Generate ECDSA P-256 key pair (NIST curve)
- Private key stored securely (GitHub Secrets: \`PT_SIGNING_KEY\`)
- Public key embedded in installer and pt binary
- Key rotation procedure documented (with transition period)
- Multiple key support for rotation

### 2. Release Signing Workflow
- Sign all binaries during release (GitHub Actions)
- Generate \`.sig\` file per binary (DER or PEM format)
- Include public key fingerprint in \`checksums.txt\`
- GitHub Actions job for signing (isolated step)
- Signing happens after build, before upload

### 3. Verification in Installer
- Default: verify signatures (\`--verify\` default on)
- \`--skip-verify\` flag to bypass (with prominent warning)
- Clear error message on verification failure
- Fallback to checksum-only with warning if sig missing
- Support multiple public keys (for rotation)

### 4. Verification in Self-Update
- Verify signature before replacing binary
- Abort update on verification failure
- Log verification result to audit log
- No bypass option (security critical)

### 5. Key Distribution
- Primary: Embedded in binary/installer
- Secondary: \`https://process-triage.example.com/.well-known/pt-signing-key\`
- Tertiary: GitHub releases page description
- Verify key fingerprint matches expected

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/src/install/signature_test.rs\`
- **Coverage target**: 100% for signature verification (security critical)
- Test cases:
  - Valid signature verification passes
  - Invalid signature verification fails
  - Wrong key verification fails
  - Corrupted signature detection
  - Missing signature handling
  - Key rotation with multiple valid keys
  - Signature format parsing (DER and PEM)

### Integration Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/tests/signature_integration.rs\`
- Test cases:
  - Sign and verify roundtrip with test keys
  - Verify real release signatures (from test artifacts)
  - Key fetching from URL (mock server)
  - Timeout handling for key fetch

### E2E Tests
- **File**: \`test/signature_e2e.bats\`
- Test scenarios:
  - Install with valid signature succeeds
  - Install with invalid signature fails with clear error
  - \`--skip-verify\` works with warning
  - Self-update verifies signature
  - Key rotation: both old and new keys work during transition
- **Artifact logging**: Verification results, signatures used

### Security Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/tests/signature_security.rs\`
- Test cases:
  - Timing attack resistance (constant-time comparison)
  - No signature reuse across binaries
  - Key pinning prevents MITM
  - Reject obviously wrong signatures quickly

### Fuzz Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/fuzz/signature_fuzz.rs\`
- Test cases:
  - Random data as signature doesn't crash
  - Malformed DER/PEM handled gracefully

---

## Logging Specifications

### Structured Log Events
| Event | Level | Fields | Purpose |
|-------|-------|--------|---------|
| \`signature.verify_start\` | DEBUG | binary_path, sig_path | Verification begins |
| \`signature.key_load\` | DEBUG | key_source, key_fingerprint | Key loaded |
| \`signature.verify_pass\` | INFO | key_fingerprint | Verification succeeded |
| \`signature.verify_fail\` | ERROR | reason, key_fingerprint | Verification failed |
| \`signature.skip\` | WARN | reason | Verification skipped |
| \`signature.missing\` | WARN | expected_path | Signature file missing |

---

## Error Handling

### Failure Modes
| Scenario | Detection | Recovery | User Message |
|----------|-----------|----------|--------------|
| Signature mismatch | Crypto verify fails | Abort install | "SECURITY: Signature verification failed! Binary may be tampered." |
| Signature missing | .sig file not found | Warn and optionally proceed | "Warning: No signature found. Use --skip-verify to proceed (not recommended)." |
| Key fetch failed | HTTP error | Use embedded key | "Could not fetch signing key. Using embedded key." |
| Unknown key | Fingerprint mismatch | Abort | "Unknown signing key. Binary may be from untrusted source." |

### Security Responses
- Any verification failure: LOG at ERROR level
- Tampered binary: EXIT immediately, no fallback
- User must explicitly opt out with --skip-verify

---

## Performance Targets
- Signature verification: < 50ms
- Key loading: < 10ms (embedded), < 500ms (network)
- No verification overhead during normal operation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Key pair generated and secured (GitHub Secrets)
- [ ] Release workflow signs all binaries
- [ ] \`.sig\` files published with releases
- [ ] Installer verifies signatures by default
- [ ] Self-update verifies signatures (no bypass)
- [ ] Documentation for key management
- [ ] Key rotation procedure documented
- [ ] Unit tests pass with 100% coverage on crypto
- [ ] Security tests pass
- [ ] E2E tests pass in CI

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Release automation (process_triage-aip)
- Blocks: Production installer deployment
- Crypto library: ring or p256 crate","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltBeaver","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:55:12.540573703Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-04T06:15:26.664659726Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-ica.1.1","depends_on_id":"process_triage-ica.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-ica.1.2","title":"Implement rollback-safe self-update mechanism","description":"## Overview
Implement self-update mechanism with automatic rollback on failure.

## Background
Self-update can fail mid-flight leaving a corrupted binary. Rollback ensures users can recover to a working state.

## Scope

### 1. Backup Before Update
- Copy current binary to \`~/.cache/process_triage/rollback/pt-core-<version>\`
- Store metadata: version, timestamp, checksum
- Retain last 3 versions for rollback
- Cleanup old backups on successful update

### 2. Atomic Update
- Download to temp file first
- Verify checksum and signature before replace
- Use rename() for atomic replacement (same filesystem)
- Fallback: copy + unlink for cross-filesystem

### 3. Post-Update Verification
- Run \`pt-core --version\` after update
- Parse version output, compare to expected
- Verify basic functionality: \`pt-core health\`
- Timeout: 5 seconds for verification

### 4. Automatic Rollback
- Trigger: Version check fails OR health check fails
- Restore backup binary atomically
- Log rollback reason and details
- Exit with error code, clear message

### 5. Manual Rollback
- \`pt update --rollback\`: Restore previous version
- \`pt update --rollback <version>\`: Restore specific version
- \`pt update --list-backups\`: Show available versions
- Interactive confirmation before rollback

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/src/install/rollback_test.rs\`
- **Coverage target**: 95% for rollback logic
- Test cases:
  - Backup creation with correct metadata
  - Backup cleanup keeps last N versions
  - Atomic rename succeeds
  - Cross-filesystem copy fallback works
  - Version verification parsing
  - Rollback restoration correctness
  - Old backup pruning

### Integration Tests
- **File**: \`crates/pt-core/tests/rollback_integration.rs\`
- Test cases:
  - Full update + rollback cycle
  - Update failure triggers automatic rollback
  - Manual rollback to specific version
  - Rollback with corrupted backup (error handling)
  - Concurrent update protection

### E2E Tests
- **File**: \`test/rollback_e2e.bats\`
- Test scenarios:
  - \`pt update\` with valid new version succeeds
  - \`pt update\` with corrupted download triggers rollback
  - \`pt update --rollback\` restores previous version
  - \`pt update --list-backups\` shows available versions
  - Rollback works after simulated crash (kill -9 mid-update)
- **Artifact logging**: Backup paths, version changes, rollback events

### Crash Recovery Tests
- **File**: \`test/rollback_crash.bats\`
- Test scenarios:
  - Simulate crash during download: no change
  - Simulate crash during replace: rollback works
  - Simulate crash during verification: rollback works
  - Power loss simulation: recovery on next run

---

## Logging Specifications

### Structured Log Events
| Event | Level | Fields | Purpose |
|-------|-------|--------|---------|
| \`update.backup_start\` | INFO | current_version, backup_path | Backup begins |
| \`update.backup_complete\` | INFO | backup_path, size_bytes | Backup done |
| \`update.download_complete\` | INFO | new_version, size_bytes | Download done |
| \`update.verify_start\` | DEBUG | checks | Verification begins |
| \`update.verify_pass\` | INFO | version | Verification passed |
| \`update.verify_fail\` | ERROR | reason, expected, actual | Verification failed |
| \`update.rollback_start\` | WARN | reason, target_version | Rollback begins |
| \`update.rollback_complete\` | INFO | restored_version | Rollback done |
| \`update.cleanup\` | DEBUG | removed_backups | Old backups removed |

---

## Error Handling

### Failure Modes
| Scenario | Detection | Recovery | User Message |
|----------|-----------|----------|--------------|
| Download corrupt | Checksum mismatch | Abort, keep current | "Download corrupted. Update aborted." |
| Replace failed | rename() error | Keep temp, retry | "Could not replace binary. Retrying..." |
| New version broken | Health check fails | Auto rollback | "New version failed health check. Rolling back..." |
| No backup available | Backup missing | Error, no rollback | "No backup available for rollback." |
| Rollback failed | Restore error | Manual instructions | "Rollback failed. Manual recovery: INSTRUCTIONS" |

### Recovery Instructions
If all automatic recovery fails, provide clear manual steps:
1. Download known good version from GitHub releases
2. Replace binary manually: \`cp pt-core ~/.local/bin/\`
3. Verify: \`pt-core --version\`

---

## Performance Targets
- Backup creation: < 1s
- Verification: < 5s
- Rollback: < 1s
- Total update overhead: < 10s beyond download

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Backup created before every update
- [ ] Atomic replacement works (same filesystem)
- [ ] Cross-filesystem update works
- [ ] Post-update verification catches broken binary
- [ ] Automatic rollback on failure
- [ ] Manual rollback command works
- [ ] Backup cleanup keeps last 3 versions
- [ ] Unit tests pass with 95%+ coverage
- [ ] E2E tests pass including crash scenarios
